div.head: The Ambassador in Italy (Phillips) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 71. For Welles.Sumner Welles, Under Secretary of State. I learn from KirkAlexander C. Kirk, Chargé in Germany. that Attolico, Italian Ambassador Berlin, has called upon him to tell him of a recent conversation which the President had with ColonnaAscanio dei principi Colonna, Italian Ambassador in the United States. during which the President, among other things, emphasized in particular the change of attitude in the United States towards Italy and the part which neutral countries might play in the interests of peace. Attolico added that he assumed CianoGaleazzo Ciano, Italian Minister for Foreign Affairs. had discussed it with me. It seems clear, therefore, that the Italian Government regards this conversation as of sufficient importance to circularize it among its representatives in third countries.

You will appreciate, I know, that it would have been helpful if I had been advised of this conversation but perhaps it is not too late even now to authorize me to refer to it sympathetically when I see Ciano.
